extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, which requires all 10 conditions including renal_impairment. rec_drug_b is set true only by r3, which requires renal_impairment. Since r2's condition is a strict superset that includes renal_impairment, any input satisfying r2 also satisfies r3. r2 targets rec_drug_a and r3 targets rec_drug_b, so they operate on different outputs and do not compete under per-output conflict resolution; both fire. Setting all 10 of r2's required conditions true (asthma false) makes r2 set rec_drug_a := true and r3 set rec_drug_b := true simultaneously, so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are both true. This violates the mutual-exclusion property. asthma is irrelevant (it only affects rec_drug_c); set false for minimality.
